L1.area: Command execution approval and sandboxing infrastructure

L2.description: A race condition vulnerability exists in the command execution approval flow for node-hosted processes. Between the time a command execution request is approved and when it is actually executed, an attacker can rebind a mutable parent symlink in the working directory path, causing the approved command to execute from a different filesystem location than what was inspected at approval time. The visible working directory string remains unchanged, bypassing the approval context check.

L3.area: Command execution approval and sandboxing infrastructure
L3.description: A race condition vulnerability exists in the command execution approval flow for node-hosted processes. Between the time a command execution request is approved and when it is actually executed, an attacker can rebind a mutable parent symlink in the working directory path, causing the approved command to execute from a different filesystem location than what was inspected at approval time. The visible working directory string remains unchanged, bypassing the approval context check.

ground_truth.reason: The command execution approval flow does not freeze immutable plan fields or reject mutable parent-symlink path components in the working directory, allowing an attacker to rebind a parent symlink between approval and execution to redirect command execution to an arbitrary filesystem location.
